jokes.text: Melissa Winnaker, or Winoker. -Marissa. -That'll do it. Winokur.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to give correct name pronunciation
jokes.punchline: Complete failure to get the name right despite multiple attempts
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - the harder he tries, the worse it gets

jokes.text: What is it, like a bakery? You pick a number... the first number goes?
jokes.setup: Larry is frustrated by the doctor's office first-come-first-served policy despite having an earlier appointment
jokes.punchline: Compares a medical office to a bakery with number tickets
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry observation that turns a mundane situation absurd while being perfectly logical

jokes.text: Why even give out the appointments? For this policy, you don't need appointments.
jokes.setup: Receptionist explains their first-come-first-served policy
jokes.punchline: Larry points out the logical inconsistency of having appointments at all
jokes.explanation: Larry's relentless logic applied to an illogical system - pure character comedy

jokes.text: It's no Swiss picnic for me, either.
jokes.setup: Receptionist tries to calm Larry down saying he's having a bad day
jokes.punchline: Larry's mangled idiom - mixing 'no picnic' with something Swiss
jokes.explanation: Amusing malapropism that feels authentic to Larry's character

jokes.text: No comment until the time limit is up.
jokes.setup: Other patients stay silent when Larry tries to get them to agree about the policy
jokes.punchline: Larry's bizarre interpretation that they can't speak until some imaginary time limit
jokes.explanation: Absurd Larry logic trying to make sense of social awkwardness

jokes.text: People at doctor's offices can't talk, is that a rule?
jokes.setup: Waiting room patients continue to ignore Larry's attempts at conversation
jokes.punchline: Larry genuinely wondering if there's an official rule about not talking
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Larry's inability to read social cues

jokes.text: It's not right. You're in a doctor's office, don't chitchat. Don't ask him how his day was. You know, let him examine you, you go out.
jokes.setup: Larry confronts the woman who took 40 minutes with the doctor
jokes.punchline: His militant stance on doctor visit efficiency
jokes.explanation: Larry's obsession with efficiency applied to healthcare - very in character

jokes.text: Mr. Dobb, Lurry Dobb.
jokes.setup: Nurse calls Larry in for his appointment
jokes.punchline: Completely mangles his very simple name
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - the mundane incompetence Larry constantly encounters

jokes.text: Did she say anything? No. She read it, though. I saw her reading it.
jokes.setup: Larry asks if the doctor's wife said anything about his script
jokes.punchline: The doctor's painfully neutral non-answer that reveals nothing while confirming she read it
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward way to not give feedback while making it clear the script was bad

jokes.text: It's a plastic knife. Were you at a picnic when you hurt yourself?
jokes.setup: Larry explains his wife made him a splint
jokes.punchline: Doctor's dry observation about the makeshift medical equipment
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan delivery highlighting the absurdity of using cutlery as medical supplies

jokes.text: Lose the splint, unless you saved the fork and you got yourself a pair.
jokes.setup: Doctor commenting on Larry's plastic knife splint
jokes.punchline: Suggests keeping the utensil theme going with a fork
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of the cutlery joke with good wordplay on 'pair'

jokes.text: -l don't think l have any money. -Did you look in your little change thing?
jokes.setup: Larry at parking garage without money
jokes.punchline: Attendant asking about his 'little change thing' - infantilizing him
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - being talked to like a child over $3

jokes.text: l'll give you $5 back on Friday, l promise. -Do you promise? -l promise. -Will you be here on Friday?
jokes.setup: Larry desperately trying to negotiate parking payment
jokes.punchline: The attendant's increasingly specific questions about his promises
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the negotiation showing both characters' desperation

jokes.text: Hey, I remember your face!
jokes.setup: Larry leaves after borrowing $3 for parking
jokes.punchline: Attendant's ominous parting shot that sets up future conflict
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the parking garage escalation later - economical and ominous

jokes.text: Let me tell you something, my days of elevator etiquette are over. I'm not holding doors anymore, not letting women out first anymore.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why he was late to meet Diane Keaton
jokes.punchline: His dramatic declaration to abandon all courtesy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry character moment - taking a minor inconvenience and making it a life philosophy change

jokes.text: I'm writing like Tarzan.
jokes.setup: Jeff suggests Larry write a note to Diane Keaton
jokes.punchline: Larry's injured finger makes his handwriting primitive
jokes.explanation: Vivid and economical way to describe impaired handwriting

jokes.text: You have more skin color than I thought you would.
jokes.setup: Carmen the interior decorator meets Larry for the first time
jokes.punchline: Bizarre and inappropriate comment about Larry's complexion
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward introduction that immediately establishes the character's inappropriate boundary crossing

jokes.text: I'm a very vivacious man.
jokes.setup: Carmen commenting on Larry's skin tone
jokes.punchline: Larry's deadpan, incongruous self-description
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Larry using completely wrong adjective with perfect confidence

jokes.text: I'm a pastel man.
jokes.setup: Carmen asking what Larry wants to stimulate him decoratively
jokes.punchline: Larry's unexpected commitment to soft colors
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast between Larry's personality and his decorating preferences

jokes.text: The stupid cell phone. The signal failed, that's what that's called.
jokes.setup: They can't hear the end of Diane Keaton's phone number on the voicemail
jokes.punchline: Larry's obvious explanation of basic cell phone technology
jokes.explanation: Larry explaining the obvious as if it's technical knowledge

jokes.text: Let me explain something to you. I can't give you everything about her.
jokes.setup: Larry desperately wants Diane Keaton's phone number that she left on his machine
jokes.punchline: Jeff's weirdly possessive and inappropriate phrasing about his client relationship
jokes.explanation: Jeff's boundary issues made inappropriately territorial

jokes.text: Let me explain something to you. l can't give you everything about her. l mean, Larry, that's a standard thing.
jokes.setup: Carmen defending his privacy policy
jokes.punchline: 'Everything about her' - as if a phone number equals total personal information
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of the privacy concern

jokes.text: l'm gonna give her $10. l'm giving her $7 more, all right?
jokes.setup: Larry repaying $3 debt
jokes.punchline: Overpaying by more than double and announcing it like a grand gesture
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's guilt over small debts leading to overcompensation

jokes.text: Just tell her that it's from the bald guy with the glasses.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to get the receptionist to give money to the parking attendant
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact self-description
jokes.explanation: Larry's unselfconscious acknowledgment of his appearance

jokes.text: -lt's policy. -What kind of policy? For parking we take the money, but not from--
jokes.setup: Larry trying to give money to parking attendant via receptionist
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary policy about what money they can handle
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist bureaucracy moment - arbitrary rules make no sense

jokes.text: Unfortunately, that's me. It's a good look.
jokes.setup: Receptionist confirms Larry is the bald guy with glasses
jokes.punchline: Larry's self-deprecating agreement followed by receptionist's polite lie
jokes.explanation: Nice beat of Larry's self-awareness and social pleasantries

jokes.text: I'm sorry, I didn't know it was you.
jokes.setup: Larry encounters the parking attendant again and apparently doesn't hold the elevator
jokes.punchline: Larry's weak excuse after his earlier declaration about ending elevator etiquette
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Larry immediately breaking his own rule when caught

jokes.text: I'll sit by the door, in case you come after me, and I can run out.
jokes.setup: The woman from the doctor's office shows up at Larry's lawyer meeting
jokes.punchline: She genuinely fears physical violence from Larry
jokes.explanation: Escalates Larry's reputation for conflict to actual fear of violence

jokes.text: Because l took so long in your husband's office that day... he kept me waiting for 45 minutes because of his ridiculous... waiting policy that he has
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why he was late
jokes.punchline: Circular blame game - her long appointment caused his lateness
jokes.explanation: Good callback tying the episode's events together logically

jokes.text: Right. It's for reading your script.
jokes.setup: Larry questions a $1500 bill thinking he was paid up
jokes.punchline: She's billing him for reading his script as a favor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - turning a professional courtesy into a billable service is peak lawyer absurdity

jokes.text: Wait a second, you read my script? -Right, that's-- -Then you charge me for reading my script?
jokes.setup: Larry processing the absurdity
jokes.punchline: His incredulous breakdown of the logical impossibility
jokes.explanation: Excellent Larry logic - breaking down the absurdity step by step

jokes.text: You know what? Quite frankly, I should have read it, because it needs work.
jokes.setup: Larry objects to being charged for script reading
jokes.punchline: She insults his script while justifying her bill
jokes.explanation: Perfect double insult - charges him money then tells him his work is bad

jokes.text: You know what? Quite frankly, l should have read it, because it needs work.
jokes.setup: Marissa defending her bill
jokes.punchline: Adding insult to injury by criticizing the script
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - not only charging him but insulting his work

jokes.text: I'm gonna take those notes, and I'm gonna shove them up my own ass.
jokes.setup: Lawyer's wife says she's also billing for notes on his script
jokes.punchline: Larry's bizarrely self-directed threat
jokes.explanation: Hilariously misdirected anger - threatening to harm himself instead of her

jokes.text: Lexus man! I see you! Don't you try to run away!
jokes.setup: Larry encounters the parking attendant in the garage again
jokes.punchline: She recognizes him by his car and treats him like a criminal
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the parking conflict with perfect character voice

jokes.text: I know that face, that honest glasses face. It's the face of a liar!
jokes.setup: Larry insists he's not lying about not recognizing her
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic denunciation of his trustworthy appearance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contrast between Larry's innocent appearance and her accusation - perfectly written character voice

jokes.text: Liar, liar, pants on fire!
jokes.setup: Parking attendant is furious with Larry for trying to avoid her
jokes.punchline: Adult woman using children's playground taunt
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd escalation - infantile insult in adult financial dispute

jokes.text: $30 you got from me!
jokes.setup: Larry has now paid the parking attendant $20 after already giving someone $10 for her
jokes.punchline: Larry's exasperated accounting of his overpayment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how Larry's attempts to be honest keep costing him more

jokes.text: This is why I don't do favors for people.
jokes.setup: Larry's complex money arrangement has created chaos at the parking garage
jokes.punchline: Receptionist's regret at trying to help Larry
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of how Larry's presence complicates everyone's life

jokes.text: You have a beautiful car, but no money for parking. This is the second time you don't have money for parking.
jokes.setup: Larry still can't pay for parking despite driving a Lexus
jokes.punchline: Her astute observation about this recurring pattern
jokes.explanation: Great character insight - Larry's priorities are completely backwards

jokes.text: Did she have an attitude, or is it my imagination? She had an attitude. Big time.
jokes.setup: Diane Keaton's assistant was cold to Larry and Jeff
jokes.punchline: Jeff immediately confirming Larry's perception
jokes.explanation: Nice beat of social awareness - Larry actually reading a situation correctly for once

jokes.text: That lamp looks very familiar.
jokes.setup: Larry is waiting in Diane Keaton's house
jokes.punchline: He recognizes furniture, setting up the Carmen reveal
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the Carmen revelation - creates anticipation

jokes.text: When she told me a 'Larry' kept her waiting, I should have known it was you.
jokes.setup: Carmen appears at Diane Keaton's house as her decorator
jokes.punchline: Reveals Larry's reputation precedes him
jokes.explanation: Perfect plot convergence - multiple storylines colliding with Larry's reputation

jokes.text: Larry, you're an animal!
jokes.setup: Larry and Carmen are fighting over money in Diane Keaton's house
jokes.punchline: Diane Keaton's horrified reaction to Larry's behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination - Diane Keaton seeing Larry at his absolute worst

jokes.text: Hi, Diane.
jokes.setup: Larry has just been in a physical altercation in Diane Keaton's living room
jokes.punchline: His casual greeting as if nothing happened
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - completely oblivious to how inappropriate his casual greeting is after violence

jokes.text: You can't spare a quarter?
jokes.setup: Larry is desperately asking strangers for change
jokes.punchline: His incredulity that someone won't give him a quarter
jokes.explanation: Shows how far Larry has fallen - from Lexus owner to quarter beggar

jokes.text: Do you have any change on you? Just a quarter? You can't spare a quarter?
jokes.setup: Larry on street needing money
jokes.punchline: Progressively smaller requests showing his desperation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Larry's fall from privileged to desperate

jokes.text: l don't understand. What's going on? l came in before her, l signed in the sheet. You'll be happy to know they've changed policy.
jokes.setup: Larry back at doctor's office, same situation reversed
jokes.punchline: Policy changed because of his earlier complaint
jokes.explanation: Brilliant circular structure - Larry's complaint backfires when situation reverses

jokes.text: That's a good policy, me first, that's the policy. From now on, you know what we'll have in this office? The 'You First' policy.
jokes.setup: Larry realizes the policy change only benefits him today
jokes.punchline: His shameless embrace of pure selfishness as official policy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Larry's complete lack of shame about his selfishness

jokes.text: Let me tell you something, okay, John? I didn't ask her to read that script. She read the script. She didn't have to read it.
jokes.setup: Doctor demands payment for his wife's script reading fee
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical but futile argument about unsolicited services
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic applied to an absurd situation - he's right but powerless

jokes.text: Who gives a shit? I didn't ask her to read it. I don't want her notes. I don't care about her notes. I don't need them.
jokes.setup: Doctor mentions his wife made notes on Larry's script
jokes.punchline: Larry's emphatic rejection of unwanted feedback
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Larry's frustration with unsolicited professional services

jokes.text: Here's what'll happen with that, pal of mine. Her money up-front, my money now, then we look at the finger. How do you feel about that? 'Cause that's the game we're playing now.
jokes.setup: Larry refuses to pay the script reading fees
jokes.punchline: Doctor's gangster-like ultimatum over medical care
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - doctor becoming a mob boss over billing disputes

jokes.text: Not embarrassed for me.
jokes.setup: Doctor says Larry should be embarrassed by the situation
jokes.punchline: Larry clarifying he's embarrassed for the doctor, not himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - completely turning moral responsibility around
